cells.label: plural
cells.function: Number=Plur — plural endings (fused with case across the declension)

conditioning: FUSIONAL: number is bundled with case + gender + declension class; one ending = number×case×gender, so no clean number-only suffix

examples: singular is largely unmarked (zero) / the same ending serves several number×case cells
fit_none.note: the plural endings (-ы/-и/-ов) project the English Plural feature and are recovered; singular is mostly zero-marked and the case×number fusion blurs the rest — the expected gap (same fusional difficulty as rus case)

evidence_citations.claim: number is fused, not a clean suffix
evidence_citations.stat: the same ending realises different number×case cells across declensions

prose: rus marks NUMBER, but fusionally: plural endings (-ы/-и/-ов/-ами…) bundle number with case, gender, and declension class. The plural is recovered from data (it predicts the English Plural feature); the singular is largely zero-marked and the case×number fusion blurs a clean number-only signal. Recommendation: PROMOTE the plural endings as Number=Plur; note that a full number paradigm requires the declension-table view (same fusional gap as rus case).
